commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because this is a draft SSRF trust-boundary change; a narrow code defect is identifiable, but automation should not decide the final trust policy.; Needs attention: The patch intentionally relaxes SSRF handling for configured custom/local model endpoints and currently bypasses the documented explicit-deny posture.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve explicit private-network denial — src/agents/pro... (sha=64d8b8b90b0076a74059cff7847bc23178f2bea7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds exact-origin `allowedOrigins` SSRF trust for custom/local model provider `baseUrl` requests, resolves that trust per fetch URL, exports a helper through the SDK runtime, and adds regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main does not grant non-loopback custom provider base URLs private-host trust without `request.allowPrivateNetwork`, and the review finding is source-reproducible because the PR sees only a collapsed boolean and cannot distinguish default false from explicit false.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because this is a draft SSRF trust-boundary change; a narrow code defect is identifiable, but automation should not decide the final trust policy.

target.title: fix(agents): scope custom provider baseUrl SSRF trust by origin
